message: Whoever said librarians were no fun haven’t met some of our amazing librarians over at Library & Archives NT 😉

Do you know a young athlete keen to improve their sporting performance? The 2026 Open Futures Program kicks off next week at venues across Darwin, Palmerston, Katherine, Alice Springs and Nhulunbuy.

The Open Futures Program is free for athletes aged 12 and over, with sessions focussed on:

• speed and agility development

• improving movement and sport preparation

Find out what's on near you or register: https://dpsc.nt.gov.au/sport-active-recreation/territory-sports-academy/athlete-development/the-open-futures-program

Welcome to our 2026 NT Youth Week Ambassadors:

⭐Abhineet Nirmal, Darwin
⭐Arjumand Khan, Darwin
⭐Charli Trathen, Palmerston and rural
⭐Esther Kami, Barkly
⭐Esther Klerck, Central Australia
⭐Esther Stark, East Arnhem
⭐Eva Susanto, Darwin
⭐Harry Munkara, Palmerston and rural
⭐Jeremy Johnson, Big Rivers
⭐Kyrah Tye Tupaea, Darwin
⭐Tasharnii Voller, Central Australia

NT Youth Week (2–15 April 2026) is the Territory’s largest celebration of young people aged 12–25. It provides a platform for young Territorians to connect, share their ideas, explore issues important to them, and engage with their community through diverse programs and events. It also promotes a positive view of young people by celebrating their talents, achievements, and contributions.

Stay tuned for updates as our new ambassadors embark on this exciting journey to make NT Youth Week 2026 memorable!

📷 Mr Derrick Cheong and the M3 Dance Association performing at the 25th anniversary celebrations of Youth Week at Parliament House.

2026 Veteran Community Awards

Recognise and celebrate the outstanding contributions of the 20,000 current and ex-serving personnel and their families who call the NT home, and the organisations that support them.

Nominate now! Award categories include:

•     Veteran-supportive employer
•     Veteran employee making an exceptional contribution
•     Employee partner of a veteran
•     Veteran-owned business

Nominations close 27 February 2026.

Find out more and nominate at https://haveyoursay.nt.gov.au/veteran-community-awards

Congratulations to our very own Darwin wheelchair basketball team for taking out the Grand Final at this weekend’s championship 🙌🏻🙌🏻 
Do you have what it takes to be the next successful NT Para athlete? Check out our NTSA athlete development programs and register today https://dpsc.nt.gov.au/sport-active-recreation/territory-sports-academy/athlete-development

Come in spinner!

The 2026 Suncoast Spinners Wheelchair Basketball Annual Tournament starts today. We've got 6 of our finest Territory Para-athletes on the Sunshine Coast to compete in the 3-day event this weekend.

One of the most recognised events on the national basketball calendar, the tournament brings together top wheelchair basketball talent from across Australia.

Our athletes will have the support of our very own NTSA Para Physical Development Coach Gareth, travelling with them to the Sunshine Coast. The tournament will give our para-athletes valuable high-level competition experience, help them build connections, and showcase Territory talent on the national stage.

To cheer them on and find out more: https://suncoastspinners.com.au/2026-tournament/

Learn more about how the NTSA can support your Para athlete goals:  https://dpsc.nt.gov.au/sport-active-recreation/territory-sports-academy

📷 Some of the team with Para coach Gareth, ready to take off from Darwin Airport

The 1995 Cabinet Records are out now! What else was out in 1995 you ask?

▪ Amazon launched its online bookstore
▪ eBay was founded as a side hustle
▪ Forrest Gump won 6 Academy Awards
▪ Silverchair released Frogstomp

Explore the decisions that shaped our history:

👉 https://dpsc.nt.gov.au/arts-culture-libraries/library-and-archives-nt/cabinet-records

Every year on New Year's Day, Cabinet papers from 30 years ago are released - giving us a peek into the past and the decisions that shaped the Territory.

The1995 Cabinet Records are live - check out some of the highlights!

Explore the decision that shaped our history:
👉 https://dpsc.nt.gov.au/arts-culture-libraries/library-and-archives-nt/cabinet-records

Sometimes a small check-in can mean the world. Staying connected helps us all, and reaching out to 5 mates is a simple way to support wellbeing in the veterans community.

You can #CheckYourMates by:
• catching up or making time for a chat
• listening with care and without judgement
• encouraging a mate to get support when they need it

A small gesture like a message, a call or a coffee can help someone feel supported, brighten their day, or help them through a tough time. It could even change their life.

If you or someone you know could use extra support, help is always available.

Open Arms Veterans and Families Counselling: 1800 011 046
Lifeline: 13 11 14
Kids Helpline: 1800 55 1800

We’ve got each other. You’re never alone. 💙

🏆 Last chance to nominate our sport champions for the 2026 NT Sports Awards!

Know someone doing amazing things in sport? Don’t miss the chance to recognise the athletes, coaches, officials and volunteers who give their all to Territory sport. From local clubs to national and international arenas, these amazing people inspire our community and create memorable sporting moments.

This year’s awards also include two new categories: Para-Athlete of the Year and Regional Performance of the Year.

⏰ Nominations close Friday 19 December 2025 - https://dpsc.nt.gov.au/sport-active-recreation/nt-sports-awards

Fun-packed school holiday program is now live! 👇👇

Looking for free activities to keep the kids busy these school holidays? With more than 1,500 events running from mid-December until 28 January 2026, there’s something for every age and interest across the Northern Territory.

Stand-out activities include:
🎬 Movie screenings
🌊 Pool parties
🎨 Craft workshops
🛹 Skateboarding
👨‍👩‍👧 Family fun nights

Plus, enjoy a few days with FREE pool access in Alice Springs, Tennant Creek and Nhulunbuy!

Activities will be updated regularly, so there’s always something new to discover.

Plan your school holidays:
🔗 https://nt.gov.au/school-holiday-events

Today, we recognise International Human Rights Day - a reminder that every person, regardless of gender, culture, age, ability or background deserves fairness and respect.

Human rights belong to all of us. They protect our right to feel safe, to express ourselves, and to live free from discrimination.

Today and every day, we stand for equality, respect and inclusion.

Every voice matters.
Every person deserves respect.
Human rights are for everyone.

Its International Volunteer Day 🌟 where Every Contribution Matters

Today we celebrate the extraordinary volunteers who strengthen our community through their passion, dedication and generosity.

Volunteers are the heartbeat of our communities, keeping people connected, supported and active across everything we do.

From coaches, team managers and club helpers to multicultural community leaders, youth advocates, Round Table members and NT Youth Week Ambassadors, library and arts volunteers and those who support our senior Territorians every day, our work is richer, stronger and more connected because of you.

You are the quiet achievers who turn up, lift others and create meaningful change in every corner of our community.

💙 Today, we say thank you.

To read more on volunteering in the NT visit https://nt.gov.au/community/grants-and-volunteers/volunteering/volunteering-in-the-nt

International Day of People with Disability 🌟

Today, we celebrate the strength, creativity and achievements of people with disability, while recognising the importance of building a world with inclusivity - not just today, but every day.

This year's theme is "Fostering disability-inclusive societies for advancing social progress."

Inclusion is not just a goal - it's the foundation for a stronger, fairer and more connected community.

✨ Let's work together to build a society where everyone can thrive.

Attention all Territory para-sport champions!

The Para-athlete of the Year Award celebrates NT athletes who push boundaries, break records and proudly represent the Territory on the national or international stage. 🌏

If you know a Para-athlete who’s achieved something incredible in 2025, someone whose performance has inspired others and lifted our community, now’s the time to recognise their achievement. 🙌

Nominate them for the 2026 NT Sports Awards – Para-athlete of the Year and help celebrate inclusive sport and Territory pride.

👉 Learn more and nominate at:

🎸 Happy Aus Music T-Shirt Day! 🎶

Today we're proudly repping our favourite Aussie artists and celebrating the music that brings us together.

Whether it's a band you've followed for years, a local artist you discovered at a gig, or the song you've had on repeat all month - today is all about showing love to the people who make Australia's music scene so iconic.

Put on your favourite band tee, turn the volume up, and let's support the artists, crews and communities that keep the music alive. 💛

👇 What Aussie band are you wearing today!

Do you know a regional or remote NT athlete or team who has achieved something incredible this year? 

Our new award category, the Regional Performance of the Year Award is for individuals or teams based in regional and remote areas of the Northern Territory who have delivered outstanding performances in Territory-based competitions, breaking records, overcoming barriers and raising the profile of sport across the region. 

If you’ve seen someone who:
🔹 Lives in a NT regional or remote location and competed this year
🔹 Delivered standout results in a Territory competition (e.g. finals, records exceptional standard)
🔹 Enhanced recognition for the NT or made a positive impact in their community

… then it’s time to nominate them!

👉 Learn more and nominate at

A big thank you to Sports Medicine Australia  for their ongoing partnership in the NT Sports Awards. 

Proudly supported by Sports Medicine Australia – NT Council, the Karen Schneider SMA Safer Sport Award recognises individuals or teams who go above and beyond to make sport safer across the Territory.

The award celebrates those who take action through strong leadership, innovation and best-practice approaches to safety, inclusion and wellbeing in sport.

If you know someone who’s helped make their club, team or sporting community safer this year, nominate them today!

👉 Learn more and nominate: https://dpsc.nt.gov.au/sport-active-recreation/nt-sports-awards

CONGRATULATIONS to all winners of the 2025 Northern Territory Performing Arts Awards!

Last week we celebrated and honoured the Territory’s artistic excellence and community impact across the performing arts sector, spanning theatre, dance, music, circus and comedy.

This year, more than 60 nominations were received from across the NT, with 29 finalists recognised across nine categories.

2025 NT Performing Arts Awards – Winners
🏆 People’s Choice Award – Lisa Pellegrino
🏆 Production of the Year – Antidote, Tracks Dance
🏆 Performer of the Year – Lisa Pellegrino
🏆 Young Achiever – Levi Nichaloff – Yung Milla
🏆 Behind the Scenes – Creative – James Mangohig
🏆 Behind the Scenes – Production – Matthew Cunliffe
🏆 Inspiring Educator – Lynne Hanton
🏆 Impact Award – Kate Stephens – Darwin Symphony Orchestra
🏆 Ultimate Legend Award – Lynne Hanton OAM

In the wake of Cyclone Fina, the Department of People, Sport and Culture thanks everyone for their patience and understanding while we undertake detailed inspections of our water parks, sport facilities and recreation areas across the Top End.
 
We’re working with response agencies and local partners to identify damage, confirm safety and plan any repairs.
 
These places are important for staying active, connecting with others and supporting community wellbeing, and we’re committed to reopening facilities as soon as it is safe to do so.
 
Thank you for your ongoing support during the recovery.
If you need or think you will need to make use of a community shelter, please make your way there as soon as you can. You must take your own supplies with you and leave pets at home.

Applications have now opened for the 2026 Men's Place Grant Program.

We've expanded the program this year with grants up to $20,000 now available 💙

Men's Places Grant Program aim to create safe, inclusive spaces that empower Territory men and boys to connect, grow, and thrive - whether it's through learning new skills, seeking support, or simply spending time with others.

Eligible activities include:
🔧 Skills workshops
🧠 Mental health and wellbeing programs
🤝 Peer-support or mentoring groups
👨‍👦 Parenting programs
📘 Information sessions

With demand growing across the Territory, this year's program will help more organisations make a meaningful difference in the lives of men and boys.

📅 Applications close 2 March 2026
🔗 https://grantsnt.nt.gov.au/grants/mens-places-grant-program-2025-2026

Today is International Men's Day - a day to recognise, support, and celebrate the men and boys in our lives.

This year's Australian theme, "Supporting Men and Boys," is a reminder of how important it is to build a community where men and boys feel safe to speak openly, ask for help and look after their wellbeing.

Let's work together to create a Territory where:
🔵 Men feel safe, supported, and understood
🔵 Boys grow up knowing their feelings matter
🔵 Healthy masculinity is encouraged in many forms
🔵 Mental and physical wellbeing are prioritised

👨‍👦 If you know someone who could benefit from support, visit https://www.mentalhealthnt.com.au/support-for-men

Applications are now open for 2026 NT Youth Week Grants!

Get up to $2,000 to support youth-focused, drug and alcohol-free events, personal development activities and leadership programs for young people aged 12–25 during NT Youth Week 2026 (3–15 April).

Community groups, schools, councils, non-profits and businesses across the Northern Territory are eligible to apply.

📅 Applications close: 30 November 2025
🔗 Apply now: https://grantsnt.nt.gov.au/
